title: Phase A nightly gate result
description: One nightly run of the Phase A gate workflow. Written by .github/workflows/nightly_phase_a_gate.yml to tests/phase_a_gate/last_run.json, validated by tests/validate_artifacts.py on every PR, and read by the `atelier-phase-a-gate-status` binary.

run_id.description: RFC 3339 timestamp the run completed at. Acts as the natural key for run-to-run comparison.

git_sha.description: Commit the gate ran against. Short or full sha both accepted. v60.36 H6 — accepts mixed case so historic artifacts and `gh`-CLI-sourced output (whose case-normalisation isn't guaranteed across versions) validate without prior lowercase normalisation.

workflow_run_url.description: GitHub Actions run URL for debugging. Optional; missing for local runs.

all_passed.description: True iff every gate's status is `passed` or `skipped`. Surfaced separately from the per-gate array so the status binary can short-circuit without re-walking.

gates.description: One row per gate in the run. Order is the workflow's emission order so a reader can read top-to-bottom.

status.description: `skipped` is non-fatal — used for gates that gate on an external prerequisite (e.g. `npx` availability) the runner couldn't satisfy. `failed` flips `all_passed` to false.

duration_secs.description: Wall-clock seconds the gate took. Optional — older runs may pre-date the field.

details.description: Short human-readable note. For `failed`: the first line of the failure. For `skipped`: the reason. Capped at 1 KiB so a malformed gate can't bloat the artifact.
